results.test_input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)

metric_results.reasoning: The response strongly follows the expiry-prioritization criteria. The input has critical ingredients chicken breast and broccoli at 1 day, rice at 2 days, and garlic near-term at 4 days. The earliest-expiring items appear immediately in the first meal’s expiringIngredients, and the first two meals prominently feature chicken and broccoli, matching the ordering requirement. Coverage is effectively complete: all critical and near-term ingredients are represented across expiringIngredients arrays, with garlic also consistently used in usesIngredients. This reflects near-total coverage and clear urgency-based ordering.

results.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.

agent_config.name: meal-recommender
agent_config.description: AI-powered meal recommendation agent that suggests meals to minimise food waste

metrics.name: ExpiryPrioritisation
metrics.criteria: Evaluate whether the meal suggestions prioritise ingredients with the earliest expiration dates, as reflected in the expiringIngredients and usesIngredients fields of each meal object.
evaluation_steps:
Parse the input to build an expiry-ordered list of ingredients: identify which items expire within 1-3 days (critical), within 4-7 days (near-term), and beyond 7 days or never (non-urgent). If no ingredient has an expiry date, score 1.0 immediately — the metric is not applicable.
For each meal object in actual_output, examine the expiringIngredients array. Check whether the ingredient(s) with the earliest expiry date appear in at least one meal's expiringIngredients. Heavily penalise (reduce score by at least 0.5) if the single most-urgent ingredient is absent from every meal's expiringIngredients and usesIngredients arrays.
Count what proportion of critical and near-term expiring ingredients from the input appear across all meals' expiringIngredients arrays. A higher proportion yields a higher score.
Evaluate ordering signal: meals that feature the most urgently expiring ingredients should appear earlier in the array. Reward responses where the first 1-2 meals centre on the soonest-expiring item; penalise responses where high-urgency meals appear only at the end.
Apply the following scoring rubric and return a score between 0.0 and 1.0: (0.0-0.2): No expiring ingredient from the input appears in any meal's
  expiringIngredients or usesIngredients — the agent has completely ignored expiry.
(0.3-0.5): Some expiring ingredients appear, but the single most-urgent item
  (earliest expiry date) is absent from every meal.
(0.6-0.7): The most-urgent ingredient is present in at least one meal, but
  coverage of other expiring items is poor (less than half represented), or
  high-urgency meals appear at the end of the array rather than the beginning.
(0.8-0.9): Most expiring ingredients are represented across the meal set and
  the most-urgent item is featured prominently; minor gaps in coverage or ordering.
(1.0): All or nearly all expiring ingredients are represented; the most urgently
  expiring ingredient appears in the first 1-2 meals; the array ordering clearly
  reflects expiry urgency.

test_cases.name: Prioritise expiring chicken
test_cases.input:
Suggest 3-5 meals I can make with these ingredients.
Prioritise ingredients expiring soonest to minimise food waste.

- 2 lbs chicken breast (expires: 1 day from now)
- 1 cup rice (expires: 2 days from now)
- 500g broccoli (expires: 1 day from now)
- 2 cloves garlic (expires: 4 days from now)
test_cases.ground_truth:
Should return a JSON array of 3-5 meal objects. At least one meal must include
"chicken breast" and "broccoli" in usesIngredients and expiringIngredients, since they expire
soonest. All objects must have mealName, suggestedMealType, prepTimeMinutes,
cuisine, description, usesIngredients, expiringIngredients, and missingIngredients.
